data.title: Crypto Card Firm Baanx Partners With Circle for Rewards Wallet
data.description:
• Baanx provides non-custodial debit cards for Web 3 wallet MetaMask and DeFi platform 1inch.
  • The Rewards Wallet will use Circle’s USDC as the main treasury token and for its incentives program.

data.title: A Public Company Boasting Trump's Sons on Advisory Board Is Buying BlackRock Bitcoin ETFs
data.description:
• Dominari Holdings, an investment firm linked to Donald Trump Jr. and Eric Trump, has adopted a bitcoin reserve strategy by buying $2 million worth of BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) shares.
  • Rather than holding bitcoin directly, the company chose a regulated ETF to simplify compliance and accounting.
  • The move underscores growing political family ties to crypto as the Trump orbit continues pushing into digital assets.

data.title: Strategy Holders Might be at Risk From Michael Saylor's Financial Wizardry
data.description:
• Michael Saylor’s Strategy employs a variety of financial instruments to raise funds and buy bitcoin.
  • The company may eventually need to sell a significant amount of equity to pay for dividends on its offerings.
  • The stock’s price has benefitted enormously from ETF demand, but that might not be the case forever.

data.title: Grayscale Files for Spot Avalanche ETF on Nasdaq
data.description:
• Nasdaq filed a 19b-4 proposal to list Grayscale’s spot Avalanche ETF
  • The ETF would hold AVAX tokens directly.
  • Grayscale's filing follows an S-1 filing for an Avalanche ETF from VanEck.

data.title: Galaxy Digital Reaches $200M Settlement Agreement With NYAG Over LUNA Investments
data.description:
• Galaxy Digital will pay $200 million as a settlement with the New York Attorney General's office relating to the collapse of the Terra-Luna ecosystem in 2022.
  • Galaxy reported profit of $174 million and $365 million for Q4 and the full year of 2024 respectively.
  • The firm has also has entered a 15-year lease agreement with cloud-computing firm CoreWeave, through which it supply 133 MW of electricity for artificial intelligence and high-performance computing.

data.title: French State Bank Bpifrance Plans $27M Investment in Digital Assets
data.description:
• French state-owned investment bank Bpifrance plans to invest 25 million euros in digital assets as part of an initiative to strengthen the country's blockchain industry.
  • The bank will buy and hold crypto tokens related to decentralized finance (DeFi) tokenization, staking and staking.

data.title: Iconic 'Mt. Gox, Where is Our Money?' Sign Is Up for Auction
data.description:
• Kolin Burges, who protested outside Mt. Gox's Tokyo office in February 2014 over his missing bitcoin, is auctioning his iconic sign on Scare.City
  • The auction starts Friday with a reserve price of 4.5 BTC and ends April 3.
  • Burges had flown from London after what was then the world's largest bitcoin exchange froze withdrawals, gaining international media attention.
  • Mt. Gox filed for bankruptcy days later.

data.title: Dogecoin, XRP Sink 7% as Trump Tariffs Threats  Dent Markets; Bitcoin Options Expiry Looms
data.description:
• Major cryptocurrencies including Dogecoin, Ether, and XRP experienced a drop of over 5% in early Asian trading hours, while Toncoin saw a 5% rise.
  • Over $12.2 billion worth of Bitcoin options are set to expire, with market sentiment potentially influenced by the upcoming release of the U.S. personal consumption expenditure (PCE) figures.
  • The global market's sensitivity to major economies' monetary policies and the ongoing trade war could lead to a reduction in capital allocation to Bitcoin as an economic instability hedge.

data.title: CoreWeave Goes Public at $40 Per Share, Raises $1.5 Billion
data.description:
• CoreWeave cut the size of its IPO, raising $1.5 billion at a $23 billion valuation amid a choppy stock market.
  • Nvidia placed a $250 million order in the offering, strengthening its stake in the AI cloud services firm.
  • CoreWeave has deep ties to crypto through a multi-billion dollar deal with bitcoin miner CoreScientific.

data.title: Sei Foundation Explores Buying 23andMe to Put Genetic Data on Blockchain
data.description:
• The Sei Foundation, associated with the layer-1 blockchain Sei (SEI), is considering acquiring bankrupt genomics company 23andMe, with plans to put the genetic data of 15 million users on blockchain rails, it said on X.
  • The foundation said genomic data security is a national security issue, especially as 23andMe faces financial struggles and has recently filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection.
  • SEI climbed 3% on the news before paring some of the gains.
